function_grapher_keyboard_shortcuts_1_: Function Grapher. Keyboard shortcuts: 1 through 6 pick a function type, D derivative, A area, T table, L learn.
where_the_function_crosses_the_x_axis_: Where the function crosses the x-axis. At these points, f(x) = 0. Solving for the roots is essential in algebra and calculus.
where_the_function_crosses_the_y_axis_: Where the function crosses the y-axis. This is the value of f(0) — simply plug in x = 0.
the_slope_tells_you_how_steep_the_func: The slope tells you how steep the function is at any point. The tangent line touches the curve at exactly one point. Use the trace slider to explore!
the_derivative_measures_the_rate_of_ch: The derivative measures the rate of change. Toggle it on to see how the slope varies across the function. Where f′(x) = 0, the function has a local max or min.
the_shaded_area_represents_the_integra: The shaded area represents the integral — the total "accumulation" of f(x). In real life, this could mean distance traveled, energy consumed, or probability.
when_a_0_the_parabola_opens_up_when_a_: When a > 0, the parabola opens up (⏣). When a < 0, it opens down (⏢). The vertex is the turning point. Try changing 'a' to see!
amplitude_a_controls_the_height_freque: Amplitude (a) controls the height, frequency (b) controls how many cycles, and phase shift (c) slides the wave left/right. Period = 2π/b.
using_simple_everyday_words_and_short_: using simple everyday words and short sentences, no jargon
for_a_high_school_algebra_or_pre_calc_: for a high school algebra or pre-calc student, including vocabulary like slope, intercept, vertex, period, amplitude
click_explain_for_the_ai_tutor_to_desc: Click “Explain” for the AI tutor to describe this function at your chosen reading level.
function_zoo_six_common_function_shape: Function Zoo — Six common function shapes
sliders_for_amplitude_frequency_phase_: Sliders for amplitude, frequency, phase. Discrete 5-state classification. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_how_does_phase_shift_affect: Hypothesis: How does phase shift affect the visible wave?
find_two_settings_that_produce_visuall: Find two settings that produce visually identical waves.
i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
explain_how_each_parameter_shapes_a_si: Explain how each parameter shapes a sine wave.
design_note_discrete_5_state_marker_no: Design note: discrete 5-state marker; no wave score; no reveal — by design.
